title: Slovakian Roast Pork
description: A hearty Slovakian-inspired dish featuring slow-cooked roast pork. This recipe is best served with a side of corn and roasted vegetables.
instructions: Season the pork shoulder with salt, pepper, and caraway seeds.
In a large Dutch oven, heat a tablespoon of oil over medium-high heat.
Sear the pork shoulder until browned on all sides, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Transfer the pork to a plate and set aside.
Add the minced garlic to the pot and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the corn to the pot and cook for 2-3 minutes, until slightly tender.
Return the pork shoulder to the pot, fat side up.
Cover the pot with a lid and transfer to the preheated oven.
Bake for 2 1/2 to 3 hours, or until the pork is tender and falls apart easily.
Remove the pot from the oven and baste the pork with the pan juices.
Transfer the pork to a cutting board and let rest for 10-15 minutes before slicing.
